T1003.001.Name: OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
T1003.001.Description: Adversaries may attempt to access credential material stored in the LSASS process memory.

T1021.002.Name: Remote Services: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
T1021.002.Description: Adversaries may use SMB and Windows admin shares to laterally move through environments.

T1036.Name: Masquerading
T1036.Description: Adversaries may attempt to manipulate process and file names to make malicious activity appear legitimate.

T1049.Name: System Network Connections Discovery
T1049.Description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of network connections to or from the compromised system.

T1053.Name: Scheduled Task/Job
T1053.Description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling functionality to facilitate initial or recurring execution of malicious code.

T1055.Name: Process Injection
T1055.Description: Adversaries may inject code into processes in order to evade process-based defenses and elevate privileges.

T1059.Name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.Description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

T1071.Name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.Description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection/network filtering.

T1078.Name: Valid Accounts
T1078.Tactic: Defense Evasion / Persistence / Privilege Escalation / Initial Access
T1078.Description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining access or evading defenses.

T1098.Name: Account Manipulation
T1098.Description: Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain access to victim systems.

T1110.Name: Brute Force
T1110.Description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown.

T1134.Name: Access Token Manipulation
T1134.Description: Adversaries may modify access tokens to operate under a different user or system security context.

T1204.Name: User Execution
T1204.Description: Adversaries may rely on specific actions by a user to facilitate execution of malicious code.

T1218.Name: Signed Binary Proxy Execution
T1218.Description: Adversaries may bypass process or signature-based defenses by proxying execution through trusted, signed binaries.

T1484.Name: Domain Policy Modification
T1484.Description: Adversaries may modify the configuration settings of a domain to evade defenses and/or escalate privileges.

T1543.Name: Create or Modify System Process
T1543.Description: Adversaries may create or modify system-level processes to repeatedly execute malicious payloads.

T1547.Name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
T1547.Description: Adversaries may configure system settings to automatically execute a program during system boot or logon.

T1548.Name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
T1548.Description: Adversaries may circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevated privileges to gain higher-level permissions.

T1562.Name: Impair Defenses
T1562.Description: Adversaries may maliciously modify components of a victim environment in order to hinder or disable defensive mechanisms.

T1569.Name: System Services
T1569.Description: Adversaries may abuse system services or daemons to execute commands or programs.
